commands.review_summary:
The PR keeps `sessions_spawn` direct in Codex app-server dynamic tool payloads, updates focused bridge/run-attempt coverage, refreshes Codex prompt fixtures, and updates the Codex harness docs.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main keeps only `sessions_yield` always direct, and the committed Codex dynamic-tool snapshots show `sessions_spawn` deferred under the `openclaw` namespace. I did not run a live model turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the latest PR revision has no discrete actionable defect from this review; normal CI and maintainer review are the next gates.

target.title: fix: expose Codex subagent spawn tool
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep `sessions_spawn` direct in Codex app-server dynamic tool payloads alongside `sessions_yield`, while other OpenClaw tools remain searchable under the `openclaw` namespace.
- Extend bridge and app-server attempt coverage to prove `sessions_spawn` is direct in the `thread/start` payload.
- Update Codex harness docs and generated prompt fixtures; remove the contributor-side changelog entry so maintainers can add release notes during landing.

## Verification
- `$NVM_DIR/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check`
- `$NVM_DIR/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node --import tsx --eval '<dynamic tool payload probe>'`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --reviewer codex --fallback-reviewer none` from the initial implementation pass

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Codex harness parent turns can see `sessions_spawn` directly before using Codex tool search.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout running the Codex app-server dynamic tool bridge against OpenClaw runtime tool descriptors.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `$NVM_DIR/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node --import tsx --eval '<imports createOpenClawCodingTools and createCodexDynamicToolBridge, builds the searchable dynamic tool payload, prints selected tool specs>'`
Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local payload probe:
```text
OpenClaw Codex app-server dynamic tool payload probe
{"name":"sessions_spawn","present":true,"direct":true,"namespace":null,"deferLoading":null}
{"name":"sessions_yield","present":true,"direct":true,"namespace":null,"deferLoading":null}
{"name":"web_search","present":true,"direct":false,"namespace":"openclaw","deferLoading":true}
```
Observed result after fix: `sessions_spawn` and `sessions_yield` are direct in searchable mode, while `web_search` remains deferred under the `openclaw` namespace.
What was not tested: No production OpenAI network turn was run; this evidence covers the local OpenClaw app-server dynamic tool payload before Codex receives it.
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
